# LinkedIn Draft - 2026-06-23

Codex SQLite feedback logs were writing heavily enough to threaten SSD endurance; three merged PRs were cited as reducing roughly 85% of the logging noise.

Went through 40 links today. Here's what made the brief:

1. Codex logging bug may write TBs to local SSDs
   Concrete local-agent tooling risk with an actionable fix path.

2. I was looking for a side business to build and looked into the AI resume builder market, here are the gaps.
   Useful niche map for a solo builder, but still a single unverified operator read.

3. n8n@1.123.60
   Low-drama upgrade with both security and billing correctness implications for automation operators.

4. Deno Desktop apps
   Potentially meaningful packaging option for builder tools, but still pre-stable.

5. I forked ik_llama.cpp and added a "--numa mirror" mode to maximize performance on multi-socket CPU systems. Just sharing and looking for testers!
   Specific performance idea for an unusual but real cheap-inference setup.
